detailedExemplarData.title: Estimating the cost of toy stuffing for an Elmo stuffed toy

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear sectional structure with headings, tables, and figures that guide the reader
Logical narrative progression through introduction, data collection, modelling, volume calculation, costing, and evaluation
Relevant content with minimal digressions, ensuring overall coherence and reader replicability
weaknesses: Mis-numbered tables/figures and non-standard page breaks disrupt the flow
Verbose explanations and repeated screenshots add bulk without new insight
Spelling and typographical errors (e.g., âMethologyâ, OCR noise) detract from professionalism
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments consistently place the work in band 3: well organized with clear logical development, though not yet exceptional. Minor structural and formatting issues prevent a band 4. A score of 3/4 accurately reflects these strengths and limitations.

strengths: Use of multiple representations (algebraic, graphical, tabular) to support the modelling process
Clear explanation of most steps, allowing the reader to follow calculations and results
Appropriate function notation, integral symbols, and domain restrictions in the majority of instances
weaknesses: Pixelated images, missing axis labels, and unit inconsistencies limit precision
Omitted intermediate steps in key calculations reduce transparency
Minor notation slips (double terms, inconsistent curvature notation) prevent full accuracy
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands of communication land in band 3: explanations are clear and mostly consistent, notation is largely appropriate, and representations are effective but not yet sophisticated or flawless. Thus 3/4 is justified.

strengths: Personal choice of a familiar toy and link to knitting hobby demonstrates genuine engagement
Independent development of multiple modelling techniques (quadratic to logistic) shows initiative
Creative measurement methods (e.g., sand-mould for nose height) reflect significant personal input
weaknesses: Some methodological decisions follow standard textbook approaches without deeper exploration
Personal context does not consistently deepen mathematical insight
Opportunities to extend the personal thread into the analysis are occasionally missed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs personal approach and independent thinking are evident (band 2), but not sustained at an outstanding level. Creativity is significant yet not exceptional, warranting 2/3.

strengths: Reflections are woven throughout, linking back to the aim of cost estimation
Meaningful critique of assumptions and model limitations (e.g., symmetry, curve gaps)
Use of percentageâerror analysis and comparison with theoretical solids shows reflective practice
weaknesses: Depth of reflection is limitedâunderlying causes of error are not critically probed
Connections to enhanced understanding remain superficial rather than transformative
Quantitative measures of model gaps are suggested but not implemented
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection consistently fits band 2: meaningful and connected but lacking the depth and critical rigour required for band 3. A score of 2/3 aligns with these observations.

strengths: Application of HL-level techniques (regression, matrix inversion, volumes of revolution) is appropriate and relevant
Integrals and unit conversions are handled correctly with technology support
Demonstrates good grasp of curve fitting, transformations, and numerical integration concepts
weaknesses: Minor mathematical errors and misprints (duplicate powers, truncated coefficients) appear
Justification of domain choices and some conceptual explanations are underdeveloped
Accuracy is mostly correct but marred by occasional miscalculations and notation slips
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Relevance and understanding align with band 4, while accuracy is âmostly correctâ (band 3). Overall performance sits at band 4. Thus 4/6 fairly reflects a solid use of mathematics that falls short of full sophistication.

strandSelections.reasoning: Selecting a personal toy, taking own measurements and deciding on several distinct modelling techniques shows noticeable independent thinking. However, many choices (e.g. formula for error tolerance, cost calculation) follow textbook methods without further exploration, so evidence is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ.

Explanations demonstrate solid grasp of curve fitting, transformations, and numerical integration. Minor conceptual slips (e.g. treating quartic constant as 1, confusion of units) prevent âthorough with rigourâ, yet understanding is good across the whole exploration.

Most calculations lead to plausible results, and integrals evaluated with technology appear correct. Still, misprints (duplicate powers, incorrect shifted exponential in arm section) and density misuse show noticeable but not major errors, placing accuracy as âmostly correctâ.

Most content is relevant, but verbose explanations of basic algebraic moves and repeated screenshots add bulk without insight. A tighter discussion could remove several pages while preserving meaning, so the strand fits âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ.

Function notation, integral symbols and domain restrictions are largely correct; matrix notation is acceptable. Occasional slips (double âbxÂ³â, missing absolute bars in errors) prevent full accuracy, so communication is âmostly appropriateâ rather than flawless.

For the most part, explanations are clear and consistent; the reader sees where numbers come from. Minor inconsistencies (switching between cm and units, mis-labelled functions) appear but do not dominate, fitting the âclear and mostly consistentâ descriptor.

The mathematics is solidly at HL AI level: regression, matrix inversion, volumes of revolution, unit conversions, and error analysis. Although not highly sophisticated, it is both relevant and generally appropriate, meriting the 4-mark band.

The student supplies tables of coordinates, Desmos traces, sketches of parent curves and a summary table of volumes. These cover algebraic, graphical and tabular forms effectively, but manipulation of images is sometimes pixelated and axes are not always scaled, limiting sophistication.

The student comments after every major stage, identifying strengths and weaknesses (gaps in curves, symmetry assumption). Reflections are woven throughout, not just at the end, giving âmeaningful reflectionâ, but depth is limited when quantifying improvements.

Choosing an eclectic mix of quadratic, quartic, logistic, sine and exponential models, and devising a sand-mould method for measuring the nose height, displays creativity and initiative. Some sections remain routine, so âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ fits best.

The report follows a clear narrative: introduction, data collection, modelling, volume calculation, costing and evaluation. Arguments usually proceed step-by-step, and the reader can replicate most working. Nevertheless, long digressions, screenshot clutter and occasional repetition (e.g. explaining horizontal shifts many times) interrupt the flow, so coherence is good rather than excellent.

Percentage-error analysis and comparison with theoretical solids constitute genuine critique, yet the discussion stops at stating values below 30 % without probing causes. Thus, analysis is âmeaningful with some critiqueâ rather than deep and critical.

Reflections usually return to the aim of estimating stuffing cost and mention how assumptions might skew results, indicating a clear connection between reflection and understanding; however, they do not markedly enhance that understanding, so band 2 is appropriate.

Headings, tables and figure labels give the work a recognisable structure. Each body part is treated in its own subsection and the final summary returns to the aim. Some mis-numbered tables/figures and non-standard page breaks weaken the otherwise clear structure, keeping it in the âwell organisedâ rather than âexceptionalâ category.

The work clearly reflects the studentâs personal approach: photographing Elmo, separating body parts, and linking to a knitting hobby. This personal thread drives the exploration but does not consistently deepen the mathematics, aligning with the middle band.

annotations.comment: The introduction vividly connects the studentâs personal experiences in Desmos drawing and knitting to the explorationâs aim, demonstrating clear independent engagement.
The description of inserting the image into Desmos would benefit from a clear diagram or coordinate labels to make the alignment process fully reproducible.
Table 1 lacks a standard caption placement and number; adopt consistent table numbering and place captions above for clarity.
Figure 3âs axes and scale markers are unclear and the image appears pixelated; include labeled axes and higher resolution for precision.
The choice of domain for $f_1(x)$ is stated without justification; explain why the range $0\le x\le4.15$ is appropriate.
The heading âMethologyâ is misspelled; correct to âMethodologyâ for professional presentation consistency.
The methodology paragraph outlines regression and matrices in broad terms but lacks a clear step-by-step outline of procedures; consider expanding to clarify each stage.
Figure 5 (logistic) and Figure 6 (exponential) have no axis labels or units; include these to aid interpretation.
When solving for $b$ in $f_4(x)$, intermediate numerical steps are omitted; include them for full transparency of calculations.
The notation for the restricted domain of $f_4(x)$ lacks consistent use of curly braces and spacing; standardize formatting across functions.
The matrix setup for $f_2(x)$ assumes the constant term \(e=1\) without explaining why; include the rationale for fixing $e=1$.
The explanation of calculator inversion lacks detail; specify TI-84 commands or steps used to invert matrix $[A]$.
The quartic function for $f_2(x)$ displays an unusually truncated coefficient for $x^4$; recheck that $-0.0341494497$ is correctly rounded.
The logistic function notation omits parentheses around the exponent in $e^{(-0.7188x)}$; add them for unambiguous reading.
The statement âtranslated to the left; the y-values remained the sameâ misunderstands function translation; clarify how only $x$ is shifted.
The period calculation multiplies by 4 twice in $4(7.06-6.10)Ã4$ without clear justification; detail why only a quarter cycle is graphed.
The domain for $f_9(x)$ is stated but the interval in the text does not match the functionâs curly-brace range; verify $7.06\le x\le8.40$.
Table 2 spans too many rows and breaks across pages; consider summarizing or splitting into sub-tables to maintain readability.
The garbled section header â-10    AG ABâ appears to be OCR noise; correct or remove to restore clear structure.
In the cubic model for $f_5(x)$ the term is written $ax^3 + bx^3$ instead of $ax^3 + bx^2$; correct this typo.
The evaluation notes a gap in $f_1(x)$ but does not quantify its effect; add a percentage-error or residual measure to strengthen the critique.
When modeling the arm vertically, the student maintains the same width but does not discuss how vertical scaling might affect coordinate accuracy.
The gradient calculation for $f_6(x)$ omits discussion of significant figures and rounding; specify the level carried through to maintain consistency.
The exponential coefficient in $f_7(x)$ is abbreviated as 0.8998001 but the regression output shows more digits; include the full value or state rounding rule.
Amplitude $a$ for $f_8(x)$ is computed from points $R$ and $T$ without explaining why these yield the maximum and midline; clarify the selection.
The oval volume for the nose is computed to eight decimal places; round to an appropriate significant figure (e.g., three) to reflect measurement precision.
